op.name: can_cast
op.notes: Returns True if cast is safe.
op.summary: Returns True if cast between data types can occur according to the casting rule.

blocks.text:
Returns True if cast between data types can occur according to the
casting rule.

items.name: from_
items.type: dtype, dtype specifier, NumPy scalar, or array

desc_blocks.text: Data type, NumPy scalar, or array to cast from.
Controls what kind of data casting may occur.

* 'no' means the data types should not be cast at all.
* 'equiv' means only byte-order changes are allowed.
* 'safe' means only casts which can preserve values are allowed.
* 'same_kind' means only safe casts or casts within a kind,
  like float64 to float32, are allowed.
* 'unsafe' means any data conversions may be done.
True if cast can occur according to the casting rule.

blocks.name: versionchanged:: 2.0
blocks.body:
This function does not support Python scalars anymore and does not
   apply any value-based logic for 0-D arrays and NumPy scalars.
blocks.raw:
.. versionchanged:: 2.0
   This function does not support Python scalars anymore and does not
   apply any value-based logic for 0-D arrays and NumPy scalars.

blocks.code:
>>> import numpy as np
>>> np.can_cast(np.int32, np.int64)
True
>>> np.can_cast(np.float64, complex)
True
>>> np.can_cast(complex, float)
False

>>> np.can_cast('i8', 'f8')
True
>>> np.can_cast('i8', 'f4')
False
>>> np.can_cast('i4', 'S4')
False
